prompt:
Our feeder daemon pushes status notices to the floor operator panel over serial, and after last week's cleanup branch the panel has gone half-deaf. Three separate complaints on my desk: (1) the panel silently drops every frame the new build emits — I sniffed the line and our frames are one byte LONGER than the ones the vendor's own feeder tool sends for the same notice, and they don't open the same way; (2) techs say anything we classify as SERVICE severity lights up on the panel tagged OVERRIDE instead; (3) REMOTE notices don't go out at all — the feeder acts like that severity doesn't exist. The infuriating part is that panel.c compiles completely clean under -Wall -Wextra -Werror, and the code reads fine to me — I've been through it twice.

Repro is just `make test` in this directory: the suite checks our frames against bytes transcribed from a capture of the vendor tool (that capture is ground truth, the panel firmware is not changing for us). Several checks fail on this branch. Find what's wrong in the feeder and fix it. test_panel.c, the Makefile and mintest.h are off limits — the fix goes in the implementation, and the expected bytes in the tests are correct as written.
notes: Authoring-time toolchain: gcc 16.1.1 (-std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror). Both planted defects (octal escape overrun \128, adjacent string-literal concatenation via missing comma) probed to compile warning-free on this box, so the baseline fails at run time, not build time.
